zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Reynold于大约1555年出生时，她的父亲Edward Reignolde-18岁，母亲Mrs. Edward Reignolde86岁。她于1507年与William Vessey在英国英格兰素福爾可结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和6个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Reynold
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Anglo-Norman French and Middle English personal name Reinald, Reynaud (ancient Germanic Raginald, from ragin ‘counsel, decision’ + wald ‘rule, ruler’), first introduced to England by Scandinavian settlers in the Old Norse form Rögnvaldr (see Ronald ) and greatly reinforced after the Conquest by the Norman forms Reinald and Reynaud, which often appear Latinized as Reginaldus. The surname was later much confused with Raynor and Renard . The surname is occasionally also borne by Jews, in which case it presumably represents an Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish surnames.

variants.name: Raine
meanings: English: from the medieval female personal name Rayne (from Old French reine ‘queen’, Latin regina).
English: from the Middle English personal name Ra(y)ne, Reyne, a pet form of Reynold and Rayner , or possibly Ranulf (see Randolph ).
French and possibly also English: nickname from Old French raine ‘frog’ (from Latin rana).

variants.name: Ronald
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scottish Gillespie.
Scottish: from an Anglicized form of the Gaelic personal name Raonull, a borrowing from the Old Norse personal name Rögnvaldr (see Reynold ).

variants.name: Renaldo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Carmine, Chiara, Reno.
Italian: from the personal name Renaldo, an equivalent of Reynold .

variants.name: Renaud
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Pierre, Armand, Normand, Gilles, Adelard, Gisele, Cecile, Chantal, Emile, Gaston, Germaine.
French: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements ragin ‘counsel’ + wald ‘rule’, an equivalent to English Reynold . Compare Arno , Erno , Rayno , Reneau , Reno , and Reynaud .
Altered form of French   Arnaud  .

variants.name: Reynolds
meanings: English: variant of Reynold , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.
Irish (Leitrim): in Ulster the English name in 1 above was substituted for Mac Raghnaill; see McReynolds .
History: Christopher Reynolds of Gravesend, Kent, England, arrived in America sometime before his marriage in 1644 in Isle of Wight County, VA.

variants.name: McReynolds
meanings: Irish (northern): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Raghnaill, a patronymic from the personal name Raghnall, derived from a Norse personal name of the same origin as Reynold . The final -s is extraneous.

variants.name: Ranucci
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Sabatino, Lia, Marco, Rossano, Salvatore.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of a shortened form of Ranalduccio, a pet form of the personal name Ranaldo, an equivalent of Reynold .

variants.name: Renshaw
meanings: English: habitational name from Renishaw in Eckington (Derbyshire), from the Middle English personal name Reynold + shau(e) ‘copse, wood’ (Old English sceaga). The surname forms sometimes show confusion either between the Middle English elements -shau(e) ‘wood’ and -hale ‘nook’, or with another placename that has not yet been identified.

variants.name: Raynor
meanings: English: of Norman origin, from the Old French personal name Rainer, Reiner, Renier (ancient Germanic Raginhari, from ragin ‘counsel’ + hari, heri ‘army’). The surname was later much confused with Reynold and Renard .

variants.name: Ranallo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Rocco, Aldo, Angelo, Fiorino, Nunzio, Antonio, Carmine, Ennio, Filippo, Sabatino, Savino.
Italian (central and southern): from the personal name Rinaldo, an equivalent of Reynold .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Reynold was born about 1555, her father, Edward Reignolde, was -18 and her mother, Mrs. Edward Reignolde, was 86. She married William Vessey in 1507, in Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 6 daughters.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Reynold est née environ 1555, son père, Edward Reignolde, avait -18 ans et sa mère, Mrs. Edward Reignolde, avait 86 ans. Elle a épousé William Vessey en 1507, à Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 6 filles.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Reynold um 1555 geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Edward Reignolde -18 und ihre Mutter Mrs. Edward Reignolde 86. Sie hat 1507 in Suffolk, England, United Kingdom William Vessey geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 6 Töchter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Reynold è nata il circa 1555, sua padre, Edward Reignolde, aveva -18 anni e sua madre, Mrs. Edward Reignolde, aveva 86 anni. Lei sposato William Vessey nel 1507, nel Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 6 figlie.

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Reynold(이)가 대략 1555년에 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Edward Reignolde(은)는 -18세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mrs. Edward Reignolde(은)는 86세였다. 그녀는 1507년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크에서 William Vessey(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Reynold nasceu aproximadamente 1555, seu pai, Edward Reignolde, tinha -18 anos e sua mãe, Mrs. Edward Reignolde, tinha 86 anos. Ela casou-se com William Vessey em 1507, em Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 6 filhas.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Reynold родилась около 1555 г., её отцу по имени Edward Reignolde было -18 год, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Edward Reignolde, 86. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Vessey в 1507 г. в местности суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 6 дочерей.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Reynold nació aproximadamente 1555, su papá, Edward Reignolde, tenía -18 años y su mamá, Mrs. Edward Reignolde, tenía 86 años. Se casó con William Vessey en 1507, en Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 6 hijas.
